summary: The post argues that in software and data tooling, the industry overvalues optionality and underestimates the cost of constant evaluation, suggesting teams should embrace deliberate lock-in to force focus on people and process. It extends the theme of too many options to advertising, warning that AI-driven personalization could make even the content itself variable and manipulable for each viewer.
money_quotes: The magic we’re looking for is in the work we’re avoiding.
Chooselock-in. Buy the BI tool;burn the boats behind you; set your tools in stone. Force yourself to worry about people and process problems by taking away your other temptations.
More optionality means more suppliers, more wandering eyes, and less stability.
But maybe not for much longer? After all, why show people a real tile, when a modified one might get them to buy an extra cup of coffee?
key_insight: Optionality in tools and media feels empowering but often erodes stability and focus, and AI personalization risks turning that tradeoff into a pervasive, profit-driven distortion.
